print("\n=== Rows 30-36 ===")
for i in range(30, 37):
    print(f"{i}: {ascii_lines[i][8:56]}")
</parameter>
</function>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so the RIGHT action moved the N(W) token out of the container to the right. The N(W) token is now at cols 15-17, partially outside the container. The container shape changed again (it opened up on the right side).
So the directional controls affect ALL N tokens, not just one. When I press RIGHT, both N tokens (or the one that can move) move right. But wait, the N(B) token stayed at cols 27-29. Let me check...
Before RIGHT:
- N(W) at rows 33-35, cols 12-14 (inside container)
- N(B) at rows 30-32, cols 27-29
After RIGHT:
- N(W) at rows 33-35, cols 15-17 (moved right by 3)
- N(B) at rows 30-32, cols 27-29 (didn't move)
So only the N(W) token moved. It seems like directional controls only affect the "active" N token. The question is: which one is active?
Looking at the pattern:
- Initially, N(W) was at cols 18-20 and N(B) was at cols 27-29
- When I pressed LEFT, N(W) moved left
- When I pressed RIGHT, N(W) moved right
- When I pressed DOWN, N(W) moved down into the container
- Now when I pressed RIGHT again, N(W) moved right out of the container
So it seems like N(W) is the "active" token. But how do I activate N(B)?
Wait, maybe the active token is determined by position. Maybe the leftmost token is active. Or maybe I need to use MOUSE to select which token to control.
Let me try using MOUSE to click on the N(B) token to select it.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# It seems only N(W) responds to directional controls. I need to figure out how to control N(B).
# Let me try MOUSE to click on N(B) token to select it.
# N(B) is at rows 30-32, cols 27-29, center at (31, 28)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 28}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
